text.title: aws-vpc-three-tier Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for aws-vpc-three-tier as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Single NAT gateway shared across all Availability Zones in production.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One NAT gateway is a single point of failure. An AZ outage kills outbound internet for instances in all other AZs that route through it. Also incurs cross-AZ data transfer charges.

failure-mode.description: Deploying application servers in public subnets to avoid NAT gateway cost.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Public subnet instances get public IPs and are directly reachable from the internet. Security groups reduce exposure but do not substitute for network-layer isolation. Use private subnets with NAT for production workloads.
